entries.title: Velnorca description
entries.subtitle: Species
entries.body:
### Overview:

The Velnorca are the sapient species of Velundra, a deep ocean world orbiting the red dwarf 'Varn'.
Native to the Deep, the highly pressurized layer of Velundra's oceans, they are large predators turned civilization builders, they are capable at both swimming and surface dwelling. They form the entirety of Solveyra's population

### Physical Properties:

Height: 2.7m (7ft)
Primary movement method: Quadrupedal
Standard coloration: Black and white
Skin texture: Smooth

### Anatomy:

Body: Large, smooth skinned and heavily inspired by Orcas, wide across the shoulders, tapering towards the tail, capable enough to handle 1.2gs and the pressures of Velundra.

Head: Round and smooth with a short blunt snout. No external pupils, eyes are large, dark, and adapted for low light conditions, vision is limited above water but strong in the Deep and Abyss layers.

Gills/ears: Two large funnel shaped structures on either side of the head, Underwater they flare open and function as gills, above water they fold back flat against the head and act as ears, the Velnorca breathe through their mouth/nose when above water and passively through their gills when underwater, no conscious switching required.

Dorsal fins: Two large dorsal fins sit along the upper back, aiding significantly in swimming, slightly flexible and flatten during high speed movement or raise during display/threat posturing, (Fun fact, during the mating dance a male Velnorca will flatten out and 'wag' their dorsal fins)

Spine: A line of bumps and low spines run down the back between the dorsal fins, continuing partway down the tail, mostly vestigial but still present in all Velnorcas.

Tail: Long and muscular with lateral fins at the tip, It's what is primarily used for swimming, can vary in size between individuals with larger tails typically being associated from those who have come from family who have came from deeper parts (Another fun fact, during the mating dance the tail will raise and wiggle in the air)

Bioluminescence: Velnorcas have markings across their entire body, primarily on the flanks, jaw and near the gills/ears, it's mostly passive and quite subtle, visible mostly in the dark, with the pattern itself ranging from individual to individual, think of it like fingerprints.

### Movement:

Velnorcas are primarily quadrupedal on land and use all four limbs and tail in swimming. They are comfortable standing and moving bipedally, especially in city structure, but prefer to be quadrupedal. Above water they are much slower and more deliberate given the thinness of air compared to water (no shit Sherlock), due to this most Velnorcan architecture is underwater and designed for swimming rather then walking.
entries.imageCaption: Velnorca (Ignore that it says Maelstrorca, they just look like that)

entries.title: Evolutionary Origin
entries.subtitle: Species
entries.body: Velnorcan ancestors originated in the Deep layer of Velundra (300-700km depth). Fossile and genetic evidence points to a lineage of large pursuit predators that gradually evolved social structures, tool use and eventually sapience. The move toward the surface layer coincided with the development of early civilization, as resource competition in the deep drove expansion. Modern Velnorca still retain deep water adaptations such as pressure tolerance, low-light vision and the musculature build despite their civilization spreading across all 3 layers
